concepts.lesson: Metals are a class of elements that exhibit certain characteristics, such as high thermal and electrical conductivity, malleability, ductility, and luster. They are typically hard and have a shiny appearance.
Non-metals, on the other hand, are elements that do not exhibit the characteristics of metals. They are typically soft, dull, and have low thermal and electrical conductivity.
key_points: Non-metals have low thermal and electrical conductivity
Non-metals are poor conductors of heat and electricity

concepts.lesson: Metalloids are elements that exhibit some properties of metals and some properties of non-metals. They have a shiny appearance, but are brittle and have low thermal and electrical conductivity.
key_points: Metalloids are brittle and have low thermal and electrical conductivity

concepts.lesson: Alkali metals are a group of elements that exhibit high reactivity, low melting points, and the ability to lose one electron to form a positive ion. They are highly reactive and can easily react with water.
key_points: Alkali metals have low melting points
Alkali metals can easily react with water

concepts.lesson: Noble gases are a group of elements that exhibit low reactivity, high melting points, and the ability to gain or lose electrons to form ions. They are unreactive and do not easily react with other elements.
key_points: Noble gases have high melting points
Noble gases can gain or lose electrons
